metadata.data_source: yfinance, FRED, CNN, CBOE, Multpl, GuruFocus, FINRA, Fitch Ratings
metadata.disclaimer: This dashboard is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. Data may be delayed or estimated.

vix.interpretation: VIX below 20 indicates low implied volatility and market complacency

put_call_ratio.interpretation: Low put/call ratio suggests bullish sentiment; below 0.7 indicates potential complacency

shiller_cape.interpretation: CAPE at 39.58 is well above historical mean of 17.38; near dot-com bubble levels (44.19)

buffett_indicator.interpretation: At 253%, market cap is 2.5x GDP — record territory and significantly above 'Strongly Overvalued' threshold of 135%

yield_curve_10y2y.interpretation: Yield curve is positive at +0.46%; no longer inverted. Historically, uninversion after prolonged inversion can precede recession by 6-18 months

ma_crossover.detail: Price above both 50-DMA and 200-DMA; 50-DMA above 200-DMA (golden cross intact)

rsi_assessment.detail: RSI near 70 threshold; not yet overbought but approaching

sp500_above_50dma.interpretation: Just over half of S&P 500 stocks above 50-DMA; narrow market leadership

sp500_above_200dma.interpretation: 79% of stocks above 200-DMA indicates broad longer-term uptrend participation

advance_decline_ratio.detail: Breadth divergence present: new highs narrowing while index advances

market_breadth.overall_breadth_assessment: Mixed — longer-term breadth healthy (79% above 200-DMA) but shorter-term narrowing (52% above 50-DMA) suggests potential divergence

finra_margin_debt.interpretation: Margin debt at record $1.30 trillion in April 2026, up 53.3% YoY — historically, rapid margin debt increases precede corrections

private_credit_indicators.overview: Private credit default rates rising to record levels, signaling stress in leveraged lending markets

borrower_health.detail: Defaults rising across EBITDA cohorts; companies with EBITDA >= $50M saw defaults rise from 2.4% to 3.0% in Q1 2026

pik_usage.detail: PIK introductions and interest deferrals drove 55% of default events in April TTM; maturity extensions under stress at 35%

liquidity_risk.detail: Maturity extensions (6 of 7 pushed out 1-2 years) suggest lenders accommodating borrowers to avoid crystallizing losses

bank_interconnectedness.detail: Private credit growing as share of leveraged lending; systemic linkages increasing but still manageable

shadow_defaults.detail: 81 unique defaulters generated 99 events in TTM — serial defaulters (defaulting multiple times) indicate extend-and-pretend dynamics

sector_vulnerabilities.detail: Industrial/manufacturing sector led with 4 of 10 April defaults

systemic_risk_assessment.detail: Private credit stress is real but contained; defaults still below broadly syndicated loan market levels per Proskauer

key_warnings: CAPE ratio at 39.58 — 95th percentile historically, approaching dot-com peak
Buffett Indicator at 253% — all-time record, far above 'Strongly Overvalued' threshold
Margin debt at record $1.30T, up 53% YoY — speculative leverage at extremes
Private credit default rate at record 6.0% with rising serial defaulters
Market breadth narrowing: only 52% above 50-DMA despite index at all-time high
mitigating_factors: VIX at 15.32 shows no near-term stress pricing
S&P 500 above both 50-DMA and 200-DMA with golden cross intact
Yield curve no longer inverted — positive at +0.46%
79% of S&P 500 above 200-DMA — broad longer-term participation
Corporate earnings growth remains supportive (KLA, tech sector strong)
Fear & Greed at 60 — elevated but not extreme

base_case.outlook: Continued grind higher with elevated volatility episodes; S&P 500 +3-8% over next 6 months

correction_case.outlook: 10-20% correction within 3-9 months driven by valuation compression or credit event
triggers: Private credit contagion to public markets

crash_case.outlook: 30%+ decline triggered by cascading margin calls and credit crisis

recommendation_framework.defensive_positioning: Consider reducing leverage and increasing cash allocation given record margin debt and elevated valuations
recommendation_framework.hedging: VIX-based protection is cheap with VIX at 15; consider tail-risk hedges
recommendation_framework.sector_rotation: Favor quality and value over momentum given breadth divergence
recommendation_framework.timeline: Elevated valuations can persist for extended periods; timing a top is unreliable

historical_context.cape_comparison: Current CAPE (39.58) exceeded only by Dec 1999 peak (44.19). Prior peaks: 1929 (30), 2007 (27.5)
historical_context.buffett_comparison: Current 253% vs prior peaks: 2021 (200%), 2000 (140%). In uncharted territory
historical_context.margin_debt_comparison: Current $1.30T vs prior peaks: Jan 2022 ($936B), 2021 ($882B). New record by significant margin

overall_assessment.disclaimer: Past performance and historical patterns do not guarantee future results. Market timing based on indicators has poor track record. This analysis is for educational purposes only.
